About
Talk to an engineer
CANNED MOTOR PUMP MONITORING

The assets you can't touch are the ones you need to monitor most.

Canned motor pumps are hermetically sealed, inaccessible, and critical. SAM4 monitors them continuously from the motor control cabinet — no sensors on the pump, no access headaches.

Talk to an engineer Download the technical note

Why canned motor pumps are monitoring blind spots

Hermetically sealed

The motor and impeller share a sealed casing. There's nowhere to mount a vibration sensor, and opening the casing defeats the purpose of containment.

High-consequence failure

When the rotor contacts the stator can, you risk containment loss. By the time traditional sensors detect a problem, the damage is often done.

ATEX and access constraints

CMPs operate in hazardous areas, submerged tanks, and restricted zones. Physical access for inspection is difficult, expensive, or impossible.

Monitor from the motor control cabinet

Since the motor and impeller share a common shaft, the electrical signature acts as a direct transducer for both motor health and pump load. SAM4 analyses current and voltage waveforms from inside the MCC — no physical contact with the pump.

What SAM4 detects on canned motor pumps

Diagram: Motor control cabinet with SAM4 hardware

Hydraulic faults

Cavitation, dry-run, impeller fouling

Electrical faults

Stator winding shorts, voltage imbalances

Mechanical faults

Unbalance, misalignment

Operational anomalies

Flow deviations, efficiency degradation

Note: SAM4 does not claim bearing wear detection on CMPs. We're transparent about what we can and can't detect.

Install inside the cabinet. Not on the pump.

01

Open the MCC panel

No shutdown required. No scaffolding. No confined space entry.

02

Connect CTs and VTs

SAM4 hardware clips onto existing wiring inside the cabinet. 20 minutes per asset.

03

Connect to the platform

Cellular or wired. Data flows to the SAM4 dashboard. First insights within the calibration period.

This is critical for CMPs in hazardous areas: the installation happens in a safe zone, not at the pump.

Trusted by reliability engineers at

"These pumps were always online with no redundancy. SAM4 gave us the confidence we needed."

John McCrystal, Reliability and Maintenance Specialist, DuPont

Not just canned motor pumps

The underlying technology — Electrical Signature Analysis — works on any AC motor where vibration analysis is difficult. If you don't have CMPs but operate other inaccessible assets, the same approach applies.

Submersible pumps

Underground or submerged — monitored from the surface.

Cryogenic pumps

Extreme cold environments where sensors fail.

ATEX zone motors

Hazardous areas without adding field instrumentation.

See all assets we monitor →

Is monitoring canned motor pumps a priority?

If you're running CMPs and want continuous visibility without opening the casing, we should talk. No sales pitch — just an engineer-to-engineer conversation about whether SAM4 fits your setup.
